articles.title: Paths-over-Graph: Knowledge Graph Empowered Large Language Model Reasoning
articles.authors: Tan X; Wang X; Liu Q; Xu X; Yuan X; Zhang W
articles.keywords: LLM; knowledge graph; question answering; retrieval-augmented generation; graph reasoning; WWW 2025
articles.abstract: LLMs face challenges in answering complex questions requiring multi-hop reasoning due to knowledge incompleteness and hallucination. Paths-over-Graph (PoG) is a KG-empowered LLM reasoning framework that retrieves structured relation paths from KGs to guide multi-hop question answering. PoG introduces a novel path-searching algorithm that explores the KG efficiently by pruning irrelevant branches. Experiments on KGQA benchmarks (WebQSP, CWQ, MQA-3hop) show PoG achieves state-of-the-art results while producing interpretable reasoning chains as KG paths. Published at WWW '25, Sydney.
